## Competition Overview
The Portaldot Online Mini Hackathon S1 is a lightweight, online hackathon designed for developers seeking to rapidly prototype, validate ideas, and showcase their work. This event emphasizes low-barrier participation, runnable Minimum Viable Products (MVPs), and clear demonstrations. It is not a complex competition format or a requirement for a production-ready commercial product.

### Event Focus
- **Low-barrier participation**: Encourages a wide range of developers to participate.
- **Runnable MVPs**: Prioritizes projects that are functional and can be demonstrated.
- **Clear demos**: Requires participants to have a demonstrable product ready for presentation.

### Event Background
Portaldot, the organizer of this hackathon, aims to help developers quickly move from idea to MVP demo and connect with future ecosystem opportunities.

### Participation Details
- **Open to developers worldwide**: No geographical restrictions.
- **Target developers**: Rust/Ink!/Substrate developers, Web2/Web3 developers, and builders with solid engineering skills.
- **Project requirements**: Projects must be built on Portaldot, use POT as gas, deliver a runnable MVP, be demo-ready, and keep core contracts open source.

## Important Details
- **Portaldot Native Deployment**: Projects must be deployed based on the Portaldot blockchain and use its native token for gas.
- **Demo Completion**: Projects must have a functioning MVP that is demonstrable.
- **Application Value**: Projects must have market potential.
- **Presentation Quality**: Live demonstrations should be clear, with smooth, logical flow and key points highlighted.
- **Community Voting**: Community voting is only used for the Community Favorite Award.

## Developer Support
- **Developer Documentation**: Available at [Portaldot Dev](https://portaldot-dev.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
- **Community Channels**: Contact through Discord for registration assistance, team formation support, environment setup, or any related inquiries.

## IP & Open Source
- **Ownership**: Participants own what they build.
- **Open Source**: Core contracts must be open source, and using open-source tools is encouraged.
- **Project Promotion**: Portaldot may showcase your work for community and ecosystem promotion.

## Contact & Resources
- **Community Channels**: Discord for announcements, team formation discussions, technical support, and Q&A schedule.
- **About Portaldot**: Learn more about the organizer and their goals.
- **Resources**: Access to grants, blog posts, and guides related to hackathons and blockchain development."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"12:["$","div",null,{"className":"min-h-screen bg-background","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"container mx-auto px-4 py-8"}],["$","$L13",null,{"competition":{"id":"dorahacksportaldot-online-s1","title":"Portaldot Online Mini Hackathon S1","introduction":"Join the Portaldot Online Mini Hackathon S1, a streamlined event for developers to swiftly prototype and showcase MVPs, emphasizing low-barrier participation, runnable projects, and clear demonstrations. max-w-none"><div class="text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed"><h2 class="text-3xl font-bold mb-4">🚀 Portaldot Online Mini Hackathon S1</h2>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">Competition Overview</h2>
<p class="mb-4 leading-relaxed whitespace-pre-line">The Portaldot Online Mini Hackathon S1 is a lightweight, online hackathon designed for developers seeking to rapidly prototype, validate ideas, and showcase their work. This event emphasizes low-barrier participation, runnable Minimum Viable Products (MVPs), and clear demonstrations. It is not a complex competition format or a requirement for a production-ready commercial product.</p>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Event Focus</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Low-barrier participation</strong>: Encourages a wide range of developers to participate.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Runnable MVPs</strong>: Prioritizes projects that are functional and can be demonstrated.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Clear demos</strong>: Requires participants to have a demonstrable product ready for presentation.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Event Background</h3>
<p class="mb-4 leading-relaxed whitespace-pre-line">Portaldot, the organizer of this hackathon, aims to help developers quickly move from idea to MVP demo and connect with future ecosystem opportunities.</p>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Participation Details</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Open to developers worldwide</strong>: No geographical restrictions.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Target developers</strong>: Rust/Ink!/Substrate developers, Web2/Web3 developers, and builders with solid engineering skills.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Project requirements</strong>: Projects must be built on Portaldot, use POT as gas, deliver a runnable MVP, be demo-ready, and keep core contracts open source.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">Important Details</h2>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Portaldot Native Deployment</strong>: Projects must be deployed based on the Portaldot blockchain and use its native token for gas.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Demo Completion</strong>: Projects must have a functioning MVP that is demonstrable.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Application Value</strong>: Projects must have market potential.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Presentation Quality</strong>: Live demonstrations should be clear, with smooth, logical flow and key points highlighted.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Community Voting</strong>: Community voting is only used for the Community Favorite Award.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">Developer Support</h2>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Developer Documentation</strong>: Available at <a href="https://portaldot-dev.readthedocs.io/en/latest/">Portaldot Dev</a></li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Community Channels</strong>: Contact through Discord for registration assistance, team formation support, environment setup, or any related inquiries.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">IP & Open Source</h2>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Ownership</strong>: Participants own what they build.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Open Source</strong>: Core contracts must be open source, and using open-source tools is encouraged.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Project Promotion</strong>: Portaldot may showcase your work for community and ecosystem promotion.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">Contact & Resources</h2>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Community Channels</strong>: Discord for announcements, team formation discussions, technical support, and Q&A schedule.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>About Portaldot</strong>: Learn more about the organizer and their goals.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Resources</strong>: Access to grants, blog posts, and guides related to hackathons and blockchain development.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="mt-6 p-4 bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/20 border border-blue-200 dark:border-blue-800 rounded-lg"><p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">This overview is a content summary.
